summary: Top-10 prioritizes diversifying the existing live BWE_OI_Price pump-long by adding short-side fades (E207/E208/E213), porting the winning taker-buy template to OI_Price (E202/E205) and Reserved6 (E210), and pairing high-conviction entries with the new exit families (X102 time, X103 trail, X105 BE, X106 hybrid) to attack R2's asymmetric TP/SL trap; fast-test priorities concentrate on funding-neutral and symmetric-geometry pairs that explicitly avoid the E126 paper failure mode.

top_pairs.thesis: First true mean-reversion short on pricechange with structurally symmetric TP=SL geometry — directly fills the largest gap (existing 94 shorts collapsed to baseline reusing continuation logic). Lowest trap risk among shorts per metric_critic, and the live system has zero short coverage today.
top_pairs.diversity_role: fills short-side gap on pricechange with mean-reversion thesis (not continuation)
top_pairs.expected_paper_lift_vs_legacy_p25_alone: +0.10 to +0.20 net of fees (symmetric geometry, no left-tail trap)

top_pairs.thesis: Cleanest direct attack on the R2 E126 funding-skew trap — funding-neutral gate explicitly excludes the crowded-long regime that compounded -13.5% on paper. Ports the winning taker-buy template to the OI_Price channel where 61 archetypes registered but none cracked top-10.
top_pairs.diversity_role: fills OI_Price top-10 gap with E126-trap-resistant funding gate
top_pairs.expected_paper_lift_vs_legacy_p25_alone: +0.15 to +0.25 (funding-neutral filter strips out the asymmetric tail)

top_pairs.thesis: Lowest trap risk among shorts per metric_critic — bounded perp-spot dislocation thesis with naturally symmetric geometry and mechanical funding-driven mean reversion. Adds basis/premium signal kernel currently absent from the registry.
top_pairs.diversity_role: adds basis/premium short kernel — orthogonal to taker-flow shorts

top_pairs.thesis: Direct hypothesis test: pattern_miner flagged that E126's edge is front-loaded and the asymmetric TP/SL trap lives in the long-tail wait. Pure 60s time exit on the strongest entry isolates whether the edge is real and harvestable before drift kills it.
top_pairs.diversity_role: adds time_only exit family (0/2745 prior positives) on highest-conviction entry — falsification test
top_pairs.expected_paper_lift_vs_legacy_p25_alone: -0.05 to +0.10 (deliberate hypothesis test, not a deploy candidate)

top_pairs.thesis: Ports E126 winning template to OI_Price channel paired with wider 2% trail to test whether trail family's prior failure (max≈-0.006) was parameter mis-tune. High-liquidity gate caps slippage; trail captures continuation that fixed-TP would clip.
top_pairs.diversity_role: adds trail exit family on OI_Price long — second channel-port for diversification

top_pairs.thesis: Reserved6 architectural fix — R6 channel under-represented in top entries due to over-specification. Single-filter design preserves sparse trigger count (~120/30d). Lowest trap risk in batch per metric_critic since R6 geometry is more directional than pump channels.
top_pairs.diversity_role: adds Reserved6 channel coverage (currently zero in top-10)

top_pairs.thesis: Direct attack on the R2 E126 paper-trap: 50% at fixed 0.5% TP banks the front-loaded edge, remaining 50% trails 1.5% to capture continuation. Symmetric risk/reward profile structurally eliminates the asymmetric TP/SL trap that drove -13.5% paper compounding.
top_pairs.diversity_role: adds hybrid fixed+trail exit family — direct E126 trap remediation
top_pairs.expected_paper_lift_vs_legacy_p25_alone: +0.10 to +0.20 (if kernel composition supported, otherwise downgrade to multi_tp approximation)

top_pairs.thesis: Smart-money + funding-extreme cross-signal is a novel high-conviction short. Adds positioning-data dimension (top_trader_position_ratio) absent from current top entries. Complements E207 by capturing crowded-long fades on a different feature axis.
top_pairs.diversity_role: adds smart-money positioning short — second short on different feature axis

top_pairs.thesis: First cross-channel archetype with funding-neutral gate — explicitly excludes the regime that produced the E126 trap. Three-channel alignment selects for genuine confluence; sparse triggers (~60/30d) but high precision per metric_critic.
top_pairs.diversity_role: adds cross-channel (*) coverage with funding-trap immunity

top_pairs.thesis: First archetype using the supported `session` field — exploits US-session liquidity regime. Paired with strict 30s breakeven exit to differentiate from X100 floor; high-liquidity filter aligns the regime assumption with the exit's microstructure dependency.
top_pairs.diversity_role: adds session-gated entry + strict BE exit variant
top_pairs.expected_paper_lift_vs_legacy_p25_alone: +0.04 to +0.12 (session edge net of macro-overfit risk)

synergy_combinations.synergy_thesis: E207 short fades the same crowded-long pumps that E126 long rides — running both creates a natural hedge: when funding is positive and taker-buy decays mid-pump (E207 fires), E126's asymmetric tail risk is highest. The pair acts as a self-balancing book.

Both port the E126 template to OI_Price but attack different failure modes — E205 strips the funding-skew trap via gating, E202 strips the asymmetric exit via trail. Joint paper-shadow isolates whether the channel-port or the trap-fix is doing the work.

Two structurally different shorts (basis/premium dislocation vs smart-money + funding) on the same channel — uncorrelated alpha sources hedge regime risk: basis fades win when funding mechanics dominate, smart-money shorts win when positioning-flow dominates.

E210 (R6 directional) pairs naturally with E126+X102 (60s time-only on pricechange long) — different channels, different exit families, opposite trade durations. Diversifies both signal source and holding-time risk.

diversity_audit.diversity_assessment: Top-10 covers all 4 channels and 4 of 5 exit families (fixed deliberately omitted since X101 composite already encodes BE+partial-runner and the user's live system runs fixed-style exits). Long-side still dominates 7-3, but this is the correct posture for the registry's accepted archetype mix; further short additions would require new archetype generation in Round 4.

next_round_seeds: short-side cross-channel archetype combining E207's funding+taker-sell logic with OI_Price OI-divergence — currently zero crowded-long fades on OI channel
Reserved6 short-side mean-reversion entries — R6 channel still skews long despite its 180s extreme-move definition typically favoring shorts
fixed-family exit variants paired with E207/E213 to validate that symmetric-geometry shorts dominate fixed-TP family's mean (+0.175) when applied to short side
kernel-level temporal join implementation for cc_pc_oi_within_60s pattern — currently bottlenecked at filter DSL, blocking C061's true edge measurement
Asia-session and EU-session variants of E212 to triangulate whether US-session edge is structural or macro-event-overfit
